articles.title: 'Clearly me': AI drama accused of stealing faces
articles.description: Christine Li is a model and influencer, but not an actor, so when she saw herself playing a cruel character in a Chinese microdrama she felt bewildered, then angry and afraid. The 26-year-old is one of two people who told AFP their likenesses were cast without consent in the AI-generated…

articles.title: How AI-driven procedures are improving the success rate of IVF in India
articles.description: AI is no more considered just a research tool — it's becoming a standard part of IVF procedures, making them less complicated, more accessible, and raising chances of conception as well
articles.content: Similarly, in mid-2025, in another medical first, an AI system helped a US couple conceive after 19 years of trying. The couple had earlier undergone multiple IVF cycles and surgeries without success. The AI system scanned over 2.5 million images of ... [7998 chars]

articles.title: With GLP-1 generics making debut, doctors warn of ocular risks
articles.description: Bengaluru: A 60-year-old woman with poorly controlled diabetes was started on a glucagon-like peptide (GLP)-1 drug to improve glycaemic control and ai.
articles.content: Bengaluru: A 60-year-old woman with poorly controlled diabetes was started on a glucagon-like peptide (GLP)-1 drug to improve glycaemic control and aid weight loss. While she initially responded well, she developed sudden vision loss after eight to n... [2847 chars]

articles.title: Stanford's AI Index Exposes K-12's Crisis
articles.description: Stanford's 2026 AI Index reveals four in five K-12 students now use AI for school while just 6% of teachers report clear school policies.
articles.content: When Stanford's Institute for Human-Centered AI released its 2026 AI Index Report on April 13, the headlines mostly went to the model benchmark battles, the $581.7 billion in global corporate AI investment, and the data center power numbers. Buried i... [7391 chars]

articles.title: ServiceNow CEO Says AI Fear Is Missing Real Story: Customers Are Spending More
articles.description: ServiceNow says AI is boosting demand and productivity, not cutting jobs, as enterprise spending accelerates.
articles.content:
The enterprise software company said customers are moving from AI experiments into broader deployments, according to the ServiceNow earnings call transcript.

articles.title: Stream your slop while you drive.
articles.description:
Suno is available on Android Auto, and CarPlay support is in the works. Now you can stream all your illicit AI covers on your road trip from Hell. According to a post in the Suno Discord, features are limited, with only search, library and explore support, plus playback control.
[Image: Suno on Android Auto. https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/Suno_x_Android_Auto.png?quality=90&strip=all]

articles.title: Anthropic's Mythos AI found 2,000 vulnerabilities but won't be released
articles.description: Anthropic built Mythos, an AI model that found over 2,000 previously unknown software vulnerabilities in seven weeks, then decided the public cannot have it.
articles.content: There is a new AI model called Mythos. Anthropic built it for defensive cybersecurity research. It is so effective at finding software vulnerabilities that Anthropic decided the general public cannot have it. Instead, they are letting a small circle ... [14093 chars]

articles.title: Data centres must coexist with frugal AI in India, say experts at Frugal Innovation Forum
articles.description: Experts at the Frugal Innovation Forum emphasize the need for data centres to coexist with frugal AI for sustainable growth in India.
articles.content: While data centres are imperative, in countries like India, they should coexist with frugal AI, suggested panellists who were speaking at the Frugal Innovation Forum, organised by The French Institute in India (IFI), in collaboration with the Allianc... [545 chars]
